Dr. Sabena Hussain-no echo at this visit, no changes to meds, Recommendations, “He has mild systolic dysfunction and diastolic dysfunction, which is typical of DMD-associated cardiomyopathy. Anesthesia should know about his dysfunction so that he does not receive too much fluid and would have low threshold to diurese while recovering if he appears edematous or has pulmonary edema afterwards. I do not think his heart is a contraindication to proceeding with surgery. The primary team should also resume his cardiac medications as soon as they are able. The cardiology consult team can be contacted when he is inpatient for additional guidance if necessary.”

-last echo 10/9/24- Shows EF of 47%, diastolic dysfunction and myocardial fibrosis (diagnosed by cMRI). He later developed mild systolic dysfunction (EF 42%) in August 2023. As a result, his Lisinopril dose was optimized. He was also started on Metoprolol tartrate for elevated average HR
** Family asked to hold lisinopril the day before surgery and morning of surgery.

Endo last seen 69/25 Dr. Cydney Nichols-History of steroid induced osteoporosis, Takes chronic steroids-prednisone 27 mg daily for DMD,  as well vitD and calcium supplements, his dose is large enough to cover for stress. He is at increased risk for Addisonian crisis. If he develops fever and cannot keep his medication down he had been instructed to get Solu-Cortef 100 mg injection IM and then to go to the nearest emergency room. He is being treated with zoledronate for osteoporosis, last dose 6/9/25.
Neurology-last seen 4/23/25  Dr.Walsh-mother has decided not to pursue gene therapy but continues steroids.
**Plan to stress dose in the OR

Pulmonology-Last seen 4/23/25, Dr. Ryan Unruh-During illness mom uses vest and cough assist, recommended using cough assist BID, but family uses about once per day. PGS done on 6/18/24 showed mild OSA, he is fed only solids by mouth and liquids via gtube, no reports of choking or gagging.

Sleep study done 6/18/24-Dr.Sandeep Puranik-Mild OSA-this degree did not warrant intervention, normal oxygenation and adequate ventilation. Kypho: 105°, Lord: °","Yes","No","0","0","82","70","149","131","105","0",,"0","70","131","149","[""Anterior fusion/release"",""Halo""]","L2 - S/I1","Yes","L","2","S/I","1","No",,"No","1.   Anterior spinal fusion from L2-S1 with corpectomy of L3/4. CPT code 22558/22585x2/63090
2.  Halo femoral traction placement. CPT code 20663
1) Return in 2 years with Echo/ECG in our Thursday CV genetics/Cardiomyopathy clinic to see cardiology and CV geneticist
3) No SBE prophylaxis is recommended based off the 2007 AHA/ACC guidelines.
5) She is cleared for her upcoming Urology and spinal/Ortho surgery and associated anesthesia.

Neurosurgery last seen 3/19/25, Dr. Chu - history of low-lying conus with short segment of fatty filum.  This fatty filum appears less than 3 mm in size.  We discussed possibilities for management in the setting of an upcoming scoliosis surgery.  We did review there is clinical equipoise regarding prophylactic de-tethering prior to scoliosis surgery, especially in the absence of symptoms of tethered cord syndrome or other radiographic evidence of tethering such as a syrinx.  This finding is important and will discuss with Dr. Drake to develop a comprehensive plan regarding the low-lying conus and fatty filum prior to her scoliosis surgery in June.  We also reviewed the need for long-term management and follow-up in Neurosurgery for her low lying conus as she ages

Urology-last seen 4/23/25, Dr. Kaefer-ultrasound demonstrates healthy appearing kidneys without hydronephrosis.  The bladder on videourodynamics reveals a smooth wall with no reflux  She fills to 160-180 ml and is noted to have a number of large amplitude uninhibited contractions.  Her EMG suggests that she has dyssynergia between the bladder and the bladder neck when emptying. She does empty to completion. The findings suggest that the bladder may be experiencing some changes due to possible spinal cord tether.  I will leave it up to the orthopedic and neurosurgery teams to determine whether this information combined with clinical exam warrants management of the spinal cord. ","h/o depression

I have recommended pressures of +35/-35 cm H2O, 4 sets of 5 cycles each of inhalation(1second) followed by exhalation(1 second) followed by a 2 second pause, for at least twice a day at baseline and more frequently when sick with respiratory infections. Today I discussed about breath stacking and its significance. Recommended using suction machine for as needed use after CoughAssist or during respiratory illness.

I see he is not using his CPAP adequately and probably that is why they do want to keep the pulmonology/sleep  appointment with me in any case with the statin I cannot clear him so the best is to proceed with the surgery and admit him for PICU/ floor as part anesthesiologist decision for close monitoring
He would need to be extubated to CPAP at 10 cm of water and monitor him closely and go from there.

He also need to do albuterol 2 puffs every 4 hours while awake for 48 hours pre and post surgerycalled and spoke with dad, Ryan. Dad was unaware of the missed visit as Grandmother usually takes Steven to those appointments.
I do see an ICU bed noted on the scheduling comments. I discussed with Dr. Serafin and we would propose the following.

Agree with Dr. Jalou's recommendations. Fortunately, Steven's postoperative course in August appeared uneventful. We were able to extubate, he stayed one night in the ICU on RA and went to a regular floor the next day.

Emily, can you check in with the family and ensure Steven hasn't been sick recently, isn't needing albuterol, etc i.e. is he at his baseline? Can you also communicate the recommendations for preoperative albuterol for 48 hours leading up to procedure?
If so, Dr. Serafin and I will discuss with his preassigned staff. As long as he's been well and hasn't had an acute decline from baseline then we will likely be able to proceed with DOS evaluation.
Dad did state that CPAP use has been good minus the times at night that Steven claims that it ""falls off"" or that Steven may remove at night subconsciously.
Dad states that Steven has been at baseline health for pulmonary status - no Respiratory illnesses or needing of albuterol.
Informed dad of 2 puffs of Albuterol Q4 while awake for 48 hours before surgery. Also informed dad of the expectation of CPAP use and PICU admission after surgery.

Dad happy with call and is agreeable of plan.
